brand.title: ESource AI Group (Production)
brand.subtitle: Knowledge Check — CTFS for Account Executives: From Discovery to Deal

questions.question: According to the course, what are the five functions that CTFS unifies in a single platform?
choices: Budgeting, contracting, forecasting, site payments, and participant payments
Budgeting, invoicing, forecasting, site payments, and vendor management
Grant budgeting, contracting, invoicing, site payments, and participant payments
Budgeting, contracting, forecasting, invoice processing, and data analytics
questions.feedback_correct: You've correctly identified the core platform unification—these five functions are what differentiate CTFS from point solutions.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The five functions are budgeting, contracting, forecasting, site payments, and participant payments. The key is that they all share data in one platform, which eliminates manual reconciliation across separate tools.

questions.question: Which three market statistics does the instructor recommend using to open discovery conversations?
choices: 49% growth in trial sites, 60% increase in protocol amendments, and up to $55,000 per day delay cost for late-stage trials
49% growth in trial sites, 70% increase in regulatory changes, and up to $50,000 per day delay cost
58% of sponsor executives using IQVIA, 49% growth in trial sites, and $15 billion in payments processed
60% increase in protocol amendments, 90% of top 20 pharma using IQVIA, and up to $55,000 per day delay cost
questions.feedback_correct: These three statistics are explicitly called out as your urgency anchors for opening discovery—they establish the market context immediately.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The three opening statistics are specifically the 49% growth in trial sites since 2019, the 60% increase in protocol amendments, and the $55,000 per day delay cost for late-stage trials. These are described as your urgency anchors, separate from other proof points like the Frost & Sullivan stat.

questions.question: What is Gilbert's primary pain as a Clinical Operations Director?
choices: High maintenance costs and the risk of disruptive technology implementations
Fragmentation leading to lack of unified financial visibility, manual reconciliation, and site dissatisfaction from slow payments
AI pilots that have stalled before reaching enterprise scale
Cash flow unpredictability and manual, error-prone invoicing
questions.feedback_correct: You've identified Gilbert's core pain—he is the operations driver who needs end-to-end visibility and efficiency.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Gilbert's pain is fragmentation: no unified financial visibility, teams spending too much time reconciling systems, and site dissatisfaction due to payment delays. This is distinct from Reena's IT maintenance concerns or Andres's AI scaling challenges.

questions.question: What key architectural feature does the instructor recommend emphasizing when speaking with Reena, the VP of IT Clinical?
choices: The 38 million negotiated data points in the GrantPlan benchmark dataset
The predictive and adaptive forecasting model that adjusts to trial conditions
API-first, cloud-native architecture with standards-based integration to existing systems like EDC, CTMS, and ERP
The agentic AI orchestration layer that connects multiple agents through webhooks
questions.feedback_correct: Reena is IT-focused and needs to hear about integration, compliance, and avoiding custom middleware—these architectural details directly address her concerns.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Reena specifically needs to hear about API-first, cloud-native architecture, GxP/GDPR/HIPAA compliance, and standards-based integration with EDC, CTMS, and ERP. Her pain is high maintenance costs and integration complexity, so architectural specifics are what resonate with her.

questions.question: Which persona is most concerned with AI pilots that have stalled before reaching production scale?
choices: Gilbert, the Clinical Operations Director
Reena, the VP of IT Clinical
Andres, the Digital Transformation Leader
Rachel, the Site Operations Director
questions.feedback_correct: Andres is the buyer whose core pain is stalled AI initiatives, and CTFS positions as the orchestration infrastructure that enables scaling.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Andres is the Digital Transformation Leader whose pain is AI pilots that never cross into production. He needs infrastructure and orchestration, not another standalone pilot. Reena is IT-focused, Gilbert focuses on operations, and Rachel focuses on site cash flow.

questions.question: According to the course, what is the plain-language definition of agentic AI that you should use with buyers?
choices: Artificial intelligence that uses machine learning to predict trial outcomes
Software that perceives data, takes action, and completes multi-step workflows without a human triggering each step
An AI system that can integrate with external APIs and third-party platforms
Autonomous software that replaces human decision-makers in financial processes
questions.feedback_correct: This is the exact definition the instructor provides—simple, clear, and directly tied to value for different personas.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The plain-language definition is: software that perceives data, takes action, and completes multi-step workflows without a human triggering each step. This avoids jargon and translates directly to value—fewer exceptions for Reena, proof of operational AI for Andres.

questions.question: What is the Total Net Value formula that forms the basis of the quantified value proposition?
choices: Total Net Value = Revenue Uplift + Cost Savings - Solution Cost
Total Net Value = Annual Cost Savings + Delay Cost Avoidance + Product Discount
Total Net Value = Staff Hours Saved × Hourly Rate + Trial Acceleration Bonus
Total Net Value = Platform License Cost + Implementation Cost + Maintenance Savings
questions.feedback_correct: This three-pillar formula is the foundation of moving a prospect from "interesting" to "we need to do this."
questions.feedback_incorrect: The Total Net Value formula has three pillars: Revenue Uplift (recovery from accelerated timelines), Cost Savings (reduced manual labor), and Solution Cost (what you're charging). This is what enables AEs to build a number the prospect can defend internally.

questions.question: When you encounter the stalled POC objection from Andres, what is the key distinction you should make between CTFS and his previous AI pilot?
choices: CTFS has better AI algorithms and machine learning models than competing solutions
CTFS is cheaper and requires less implementation effort than other enterprise platforms
CTFS is not another pilot—it is the enterprise orchestration infrastructure that connects multiple agents and makes pilots scalable
CTFS uses a proprietary agentic framework that no other vendor has developed
questions.feedback_correct: This response turns his objection into a root cause analysis and positions CTFS as the solution to why his last attempt failed.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The key counter to the stalled POC objection is that CTFS is not another standalone pilot—it is the orchestration layer and infrastructure that makes pilots scalable. You're not asking him to try AI again; you're explaining why the last one didn't work and offering the fix.

questions.question: When closing a deal conversation, what market validation statistic should you reference to establish social proof?
choices: 49% growth in trial sites since 2019
15 billion dollars in payments processed by IQVIA
58% of sponsor executives already use IQVIA Technologies for clinical trial financial management, according to Frost & Sullivan May 2025
90% of the top 20 pharma companies use IQVIA for clinical trial management
questions.feedback_correct: This specific stat from Frost & Sullivan is positioned as directional market proof that the prospect's peers have already moved—it's social proof, not just vanity.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The closing statistic to use is the Frost & Sullivan May 2025 finding that 58% of sponsor executives already use IQVIA Technologies. This is framed as social proof that the market has made a directional choice, not just a product feature or operational metric.

questions.question: According to the instructor, what is the most important discovery question to ask before choosing which persona-specific message to use?
choices: What is your current annual spend on clinical trial financial management?
Who owns financial systems, who signs off on technology decisions, and who represents site operations in this conversation?
Have you considered adopting AI or automation in your clinical operations?
What specific pain points are you experiencing with your current trial processes?
questions.feedback_correct: These three questions map directly to your four personas and determine who has authority—skipping this step means pitching the wrong message to the wrong person first.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The three discovery questions are: who owns financial systems, who signs off on technology decisions, and who represents site operations. These questions quickly identify which personas are in the room and who has authority, which is discovery priority one before you choose your message track.
